growth_records.strain: Ethanol enrichment culture (biogas sludge-derived), six-species model surrogate: P. freudenreichii, A. woodii, D. vulgaris, S. fumaroxidans, M. barkeri, M. hungatei
growth_records.medium: Anaerobic digestion medium with 14.6% (v/v) ethanol as main carbon source

up.method: calculated from measured biogas volume/composition, biomass protein, residual ethanol at steady state (avg of 2 reactors)

growth_records.cite: Koch S et al. RedCom: A strategy for reduced metabolic modeling of complex microbial communities and its application for analyzing experimental datasets from anaerobic digestion. PLoS Computational Biology (2019). DOI:10.1371/journal.pcbi.1006759. [PMC6373973]
growth_records.method: reported (mu_c set equal to measured dilution rate D=0.001 1/h)
growth_records.snippet: Experimental data 0.59 0.27 0.87 3.25 1.48 ... In the simulations, mu_c was fixed to the dilution rate of the experiments (0.001 h-1)
growth_records.notes: Table 3, six-species ethanol community. Experimental (measured) exchange rates from the enrichment continuous culture at D=mu_c=0.001 1/h (avg of two reactors): ethanol uptake 0.59, CO2 0.27, CH4 0.87 mmol/gDWc/h. Yields CH4:ethanol=1.48 mol/mol, CH4:CO2=3.25. Doubling time = ln2/0.001 = 693.1 h. Rates normalized to total community biomass (gDWc). Temperature/pH given in supplementary S6 Text, not main text (null here). This is a mixed methanogenic community, not a single species. Additional dilution rates in S7 Text.
